Isaiah Stewart (to Memphis); Caris LeVert and two second-round picks (to Milwaukee)



Milwaukee Bucks



Caris LeVert, two second-round picks (from Detroit)



Taurean Prince, Gary Harris (to Detroit)



LA Clippers



$9.1 million TPE (traded player exception)



John Collins (to Detroit via sign-and-trade)





What Middleton and Collins bring to their new teams



Khris Middleton is returning to the Washington Wizards on a three-year, $17.6 million contract, agreed via sign-and-trade with the Dallas Mavericks on Tuesday. Agent Mike Lindeman of Excel Sports Management confirmed the deal to ESPN's Shams Charania, sending the 14-year veteran back to D.C. for a second stint less than five months after he was dealt away.The move is not a standalone transaction. It's the centerpiece of a sprawling six-team swap that also touches the Detroit Pistons, Memphis Grizzlies, Los Angeles Clippers and Milwaukee Bucks, reshuffling roster pieces across the league in a single coordinated exchange.Khris Middleton, 34, spent parts of two seasons in Washington after arriving from Milwaukee at the 2025 deadline before being flipped to Dallas this past February as part of the trade that brought Anthony Davis to the Wizards. He averaged 10.2 points, 3.7 rebounds and 2.8 assists across 63 games last season split between the two teams.His agent described the move as a return to a locker room where Middleton had already built a "leader with championship presence" reputation, a nod to his history of winning a title with Milwaukee in 2021.The Wizards are sending D'Angelo Russell, a future second-round pick, and a second-round pick swap to the Grizzlies to help facilitate the deal. Elsewhere in the transaction, the Pistons are acquiring John Collins from the Clippers on a reported three-year, $51 million contract, while Memphis is set to receive Isaiah Stewart from Detroit.Dallas gets Santi Aldama from the Grizzlies and opens a $5.6 million trade exception in the process. The Bucks land Caris LeVert from Detroit, with the Pistons getting Taurean Prince and Gary Harris plus two second-round picks in return.Middleton's peak came alongside Giannis Antetokounmpo, when he served as Milwaukee's second-leading scorer during the 2020-21 title run and lifted his production to nearly 24 points a game in the playoffs. He's no longer that player at 34, but his career playoff averages of 20.6 points, 6.5 rebounds and 4.8 assists across 80 games suggest he can still raise his level when it matters, even in a diminished role behind Young and Davis. Collins, meanwhile, gives Detroit a frontcourt scorer to pair with Jalen Duren after averaging 13.6 points and 5.3 rebounds last season on 55.2% shooting.None of the six franchises involved landed a superstar in the swap, but the trade reshapes rotations from Washington to Milwaukee heading into training camp. For the Wizards, the question now is whether a roster built around Young, Davis, rookie AJ Dybantsa and a returning Middleton can translate offseason additions into real progress in a crowded Eastern Conference.
Article Summary: Khris Middleton is returning to the Washington Wizards on a three-year, $17.6 million contract. Agent Mike Lindeman of Excel Sports Management confirmed the deal to ESPN's Shams Charania. It's the centerpiece of a sprawling six-team swap that also touches the Detroit Pistons, Memphis Grizzlies, Los Angeles Clippers and Milwaukee Bucks.

Article Title: Sophie Cunningham shares eye-catching beach moments with 6-foot-2 Fever teammate
Article Text:
Sophie Cunningham and Lexie Hull. Image via: Kate Frese/ NBAE via Getty Images

Sophie Cunningham turns heads in bikini beach photos with Fever teammate Lexie Hull



Fans react to Sophie Cunningham and Lexie Hull's beach moment



Sophie Cunningham's Fever status



Sophie Cunningham. Image via: Erica Denhoff/ Icon Sportswire via Getty Images

Sophie Cunningham and Lexie Hull enjoyed a relaxing day at Manhattan Beach during the Indiana Fever's West Coast road trip, offering fans a glimpse into their close friendship ahead of Wednesday's WNBA game against the Los Angeles Sparks. The beach outing came after Indiana's convincing 84-68 victory over the Las Vegas Aces, giving the team a brief opportunity to recharge before returning to action.The social media post quickly gained attention among WNBA fans, not only because of the pair's beach photos but also because Cunningham and Hull have become two of the Fever's most recognizable personalities off the court.Hull shared a selfie with Cunningham on her Instagram Story from Manhattan Beach, California, where both guards spent part of their day off soaking up the sun before Indiana resumed its demanding travel schedule. Cunningham later reshared the image, adding to the buzz surrounding the post.Hull wore a pastel-striped triangle bikini with bright pink trim, while Cunningham opted for a geometric-patterned triangle bikini. The two lay side by side on the sand, enjoying what Hull described in her caption as "much needed r&r."The outing followed another productive performance from both players during Sunday's win over Las Vegas.Cunningham scored nine points off the bench, knocking down three shots from beyond the arc, while Hull continued her strong run as a starter with 10 points, four rebounds and two steals in 22 minutes.The Fever improved to 12-8 with that victory, marking their second straight win after defeating the Los Angeles Sparks 111-87 on June 27. Indiana now sits firmly in the playoff picture while navigating one of the busiest stretches of its schedule.Hull previously appeared on Cunningham's offseason podcast, and Cunningham has often praised her teammate publicly. Together, they have become fan favorites, with many supporters affectionately referring to them as the Fever's "mermaids."The beach selfie quickly circulated across social media after being reposted by fan accounts, prompting hundreds of reactions from supporters.Some fans joked about the security surrounding the players during the outing."I see security is staying out of sight. Lol!" one fan wrote.Another added, "I bet Lex fiancé is there for security."Others focused on the ongoing online conversation surrounding the Fever."lol so now you like these two because you think they aren’t hanging out with Caitlin Clark in that moment? But as soon as they’re back on court backing up their teammate, the racial hate and insults start up again," another user posted.One response defended the lighthearted nature of the interactions between Cunningham and Clark."You don’t seem to grasp the irony or humor. Sophie calls CC pale as a joke. You sound humorless and missing the point."Another fan added a humorous observation."Feel bad for the body guards standing on the beach in suits protecting these two."While Cunningham's social media presence often grabs headlines, her production has been equally valuable for Indiana this season.The veteran guard has quietly delivered one of the best campaigns of her career after re-signing with the Fever during the offseason. Through 19 games, Cunningham is averaging 9.5 points, 2.5 rebounds and 1.4 assists while shooting 49.1 percent from the field and an impressive 42.9 percent from three-point range.Despite those numbers, Cunningham was left off the WNBA All-Star reserve list when the league announced its 12 selections on Tuesday. The omission means the 29-year-old is still searching for her first All-Star appearance after eight seasons.Cunningham has reached double figures nine times this season, including a season-high 24-point performance against the Toronto Tempo on June 16 and a 17-point outing against the Seattle Storm earlier in the year.
Article Summary: Sophie Cunningham and Lexie Hull enjoyed a relaxing day at Manhattan Beach, California. Cunningham shared a selfie with Hull on her Instagram Story. The beach outing came after Indiana's 84-68 victory over the Las Vegas Aces. Cunningham and Hull have become two of the Fever's most recognizable personalities.

Article Title: 'The tournament is fixed': Egypt star alleges 'clear and obvious injustice' after Argentina defeat
Article Text:
Argentina's Lionel Messi battles for the ball with Egypt's Mostafa Zico. (AP Photo)

Egypt forward Mostafa Ziko launched a scathing attack on the match officials after his side's heartbreaking 3-2 defeat to defending champions Argentina in the FIFA World Cup Round of 16, claiming the refereeing was unfair and alleging that "the tournament is fixed. "Egypt appeared on course for one of the biggest upsets in World Cup history after taking a 2-0 lead in the 67th minute. However, Argentina produced a stunning late comeback, with Cristian Romero, Lionel Messi and Enzo Fernandez scoring within a 13-minute spell to send the three-time world champions into the quarter-finals.One of the defining moments of the contest came early in the second half when Ziko found the net, only for the goal to be overturned after a VAR review. Officials ruled that the Egyptian forward had committed a foul in the build-up. Although Ziko later got on the scoresheet, the disallowed effort proved to be a pivotal moment in the match.Speaking to the broadcaster after the final whistle, an emotional Ziko questioned the officiating and suggested the outcome had been influenced by the referees."No fair, no fair, referee, no fair. Injustice, clear and obvious injustice.He is wasting the efforts of an entire country. From the start of the match, he was against us. It is not allowed for us to leave as 2-0 winners against Argentina. The tournament is fixed. God is sufficient for us, and He is the best disposer of affairs."The Egypt forward also apologised to supporters, insisting the players had done everything they could."We wanted to make them happy today. We couldn't do that. But I swear it was out of our hands. It is in the referee's hands. The tournament is fixed, it is obvious anyway."Egypt had made a dream start when Yasser Ibrahim headed home from Marwan Attia's corner in the 15th minute. Argentina had an immediate opportunity to respond after Nicolas Tagliafico won a penalty, but goalkeeper Mostafa Shobeir produced an outstanding save to deny Messi from the spot.Shobeir continued to frustrate the defending champions by keeping out efforts from Alexis Mac Allister and Julian Alvarez, while Messi also struck the crossbar with a free-kick as Egypt carried a 1-0 advantage into the interval.The Pharaohs believed they had doubled their lead shortly after the restart through Ziko, but VAR intervened to disallow the goal. Egypt responded positively, though, and finally made it 2-0 in the 67th minute when Mohamed Salah and Haissem Hassan combined to set up Ziko for a clinical finish.Argentina's response was swift. Romero headed in Messi's free-kick in the 79th minute to reduce the deficit before Messi levelled the scores four minutes later with a powerful strike that went in off the underside of the crossbar after brushing the goalkeeper's gloves.With extra time approaching, Enzo Fernandez completed the dramatic turnaround in stoppage time, firing a low finish into the bottom corner after latching onto a loose ball at the edge of the box.Argentina's 3-2 victory secured their place in the quarter-finals, where they will face the winners of the Round of 16 meeting between Switzerland and Colombia on July 8.
Article Summary: Argentina beat Egypt 3-2 in the FIFA World Cup Round of 16. Cristian Romero, Lionel Messi and Enzo Fernandez scored in a 13-minute spell. Egypt forward Mostafa Ziko claims the refereeing was unfair. Ziko also claims that "the tournament is fixed"

Article Title: Tempers explode as Lionel Messi clashes with Egypt coach, staff member sent off in dramatic World Cup scenes | Watch
Article Text:
Tempers explode as Lionel Messi clashes with Egypt coach. (Video grabs)

Messi-Hassan confrontation sparks chaotic scenes



WATCH:

Egypt's frustration boils over



Argentina's thrilling FIFA World Cup Round of 16 victory over Egypt witnessed extraordinary scenes as Lionel Messi became involved in a heated confrontation with Egypt head coach Hossam Hassan before tempers boiled over on the touchline, culminating in a member of the Egyptian coaching staff being shown a red card after charging towards the Argentine captain.The dramatic sequence, which quickly went viral on social media, overshadowed Argentina's remarkable 3-2 comeback victory and has sparked fresh debate over FIFA's anti-racism protocol after Hassan was seen making the governing body's official crossed-arms "X" gesture during his exchange with Messi.The flashpoint came moments after the final whistle following a pulsating encounter in which Argentina recovered from 2-0 down to book their place in the quarter-finals.Videos circulating online show Messi walking towards Hassan before the pair exchanged words near the touchline. While the exact contents of the conversation remain unknown, the Egypt coach immediately responded by crossing his arms in an "X" shape — FIFA's official anti-racism signal introduced in 2024 to report alleged racist abuse and activate the competition's three-step anti-discrimination protocol.No audio from the exchange has been released and neither FIFA nor the Egyptian Football Association has clarified whether Hassan's gesture was directed specifically at Messi, Argentina's bench, the match officials or another incident occurring around the technical area.The confrontation quickly escalated as several Egyptian coaching staff members rushed towards the scene, with one visibly losing his temper before charging aggressively towards Messi.Argentina players immediately surrounded their captain, while match officials, security personnel and other Egyptian staff intervened to prevent the situation from escalating further. The furious Egyptian staff member was eventually shown a red card and escorted away as both benches attempted to restore order.The ugly scenes reflected Egypt's lingering frustration after surrendering a two-goal lead in heartbreaking fashion.Hossam Hassan's side appeared on course for one of the biggest upsets of the tournament after first-half and second-half strikes had stunned the defending champions. However, Cristian Romero ignited Argentina's comeback before Messi equalised in the 83rd minute. Enzo Fernandez then completed the turnaround with a stoppage-time winner.Egypt's anger had already been building after a crucial Mostafa Ziko goal was ruled out following a VAR review for a foul in the build-up. The African side also believed they should have been awarded a penalty for an alleged tug on Hamdy Fathy shortly before Argentina launched the attack that produced the decisive goal.Speaking after the defeat, Hassan launched a scathing attack on the officiating."I’m not convinced with this outcome. I’m not convinced with the way things unfolded during this match," he said. "We have been treated unfairly today. We have suffered injustice."The Egyptian coach even suggested he would not watch another match at the tournament following his team's elimination.
Article Summary: Argentina beat Egypt 3-2 in World Cup Round of 16 clash. Lionel Messi and Hossam Hassan involved in heated confrontation on touchline. Egyptian coaching staff member shown red card after charging towards Messi. Hassan seen making FIFA's official crossed-arms "X" gesture during exchange.

Article Title: Zlatan Ibrahimović gives honest reaction to World Cup teams’ painful exits with ruthless one-liners
Article Text:
Zlatan Ibrahimović gives his honest verdict on World Cup exits with ruthless one-liners/ Image: X

Zlatan's blunt farewell to the United States



Brazil, Portugal and Germany also come under fire



Germany's exit proved equally unforgiving.



Mexico and even Sweden escape no mercy



One of the World Cup's breakout studio moments



The 2026 FIFA World Cup has produced unforgettable drama on the pitch, but some of the tournament's most talked-about moments have come away from it. Working as a lead analyst for FOX Sports, Zlatan Ibrahimović has turned his post-match appearances into must-watch television, delivering trademark one-liners aimed at several of the tournament's most notable knockout casualties.A total of 24 teams were eliminated across the Round of 32 and Round of 16, leaving exactly eight nations standing in the quarter-finals. Among those knocked out, Ibrahimović singled out a handful of teams for his trademark blend of humour, blunt analysis and unmistakable confidence.Standing in front of the national flag of the defeated team after each match, the Swedish icon has mixed humour, blunt analysis and his unmistakable confidence to bid farewell to the tournament's fallen sides. His reactions have quickly gone viral across social media, becoming one of the World Cup's most popular recurring segments.The United States entered the Round of 16 carrying enormous expectations after an encouraging tournament on home soil under Mauricio Pochettino. Those hopes ended abruptly with a comprehensive 4-1 defeat to Belgium, despite the controversy surrounding Folarin Balogun's overturned suspension dominating the buildup.Ibrahimović offered little sympathy."US is out. So your American dream is over."He then added the line that immediately spread across social media."Next time call it football, not soccer. Okay?"Although he later acknowledged the hosts' commitment, Ibrahimović concluded that the Americans simply lacked the technical quality needed to compete with one of Europe's elite sides.Brazil's surprise 2-1 Round of 16 defeat to Norway marked the Seleção's earliest World Cup exit since 1990, but Ibrahimović insisted he was far from shocked.Rather than focusing on Carlo Ancelotti, he argued that too much attention had been paid to the manager instead of the actual squad, suggesting this was no longer the Brazil capable of dominating international football.His farewell was typically brief."Brazil, you're out. The samba continues.... in Brazil. See you next time."Portugal's elimination against Spain also received the Zlatan treatment. With Cristiano Ronaldo confirming before the match that the tournament would be his final World Cup, Ibrahimović offered a concise verdict that many interpreted as another playful jab at his long-time rival."Portugal, almost. But almost is not enough."After the 2014 champions were knocked out by Paraguay in a dramatic penalty shootout following extra time, Ibrahimović reflected on how quickly fortunes change in tournament football, referencing defender Jonathan Tah's disallowed winner and later penalty miss."You need to be strong mentally," he said before delivering another trademark sign-off."Germany, it's over. Now you can drink sad beers."Mexico's tournament ended in dramatic fashion after a thrilling 3-2 defeat to England in the Round of 16. El Tri pushed England all the way despite playing against 10 men for much of the closing stages, but late defensive lapses ultimately denied them a place in the quarter-finals.Ibrahimović acknowledged Mexico's spirit before ending with another deadpan farewell."Mexico, enjoy your tacos. Amigos, adiós."Even his own country avoided no special treatment.Sweden's Round of 16 exit against France prompted perhaps Ibrahimović's strangest line of the tournament, aimed directly at his fellow countrymen."Sweden, I told you there is no new Zlatan. You're out."The comment left studio colleagues laughing while viewers attempted to decipher the inside joke.Ibrahimović's recurring post-match segment has become one of the unexpected success stories of the tournament's television coverage.Rather than commenting on every team eliminated from the knockout rounds, Ibrahimović selected several high-profile exits for his trademark send-offs. Hosts United States, five-time champions Brazil, Cristiano Ronaldo's Portugal, Germany, Mexico and even Sweden all found themselves on the receiving end of his brutally concise remarks, which have become a viral feature of FOX Sports' World Cup coverage.
Article Summary: Zlatan Ibrahimović has turned his post-match appearances into must-watch television. The Swedish icon has delivered trademark one-liners aimed at several of the tournament's most notable knockout casualties. Ibrahimovic has mixed humour, blunt analysis and unmistakable confidence to bid farewell to the fallen sides.

Article Title: FIFA World Cup 2026 quarterfinal schedule: Full fixtures, match timings, venues and semifinal pathway
Article Text:
Kylian Mbappé, Erling Haaland, Harry Kane and Lionel Messi (Agency Image)

FIFA World Cup 2026 quarterfinal schedule

Date Day Match Venue IST Time July 10 Friday France vs Morocco Gillette Stadium, Foxborough 1:30 AM July 11 Saturday Spain vs Belgium SoFi Stadium, Inglewood 12:30 AM July 12 Sunday Norway vs England Hard Rock Stadium, Miami Gardens 2:30 AM July 12 Sunday Argentina vs Switzerland Arrowhead Stadium, Kansas City 6:30 AM

The FIFA World Cup 2026 has reached its business end, with only eight teams remaining in the race for football's biggest prize. Following a dramatic Round of 16 that produced several memorable contests and major upsets, attention now turns to the quarterfinals, where four high-stakes clashes will decide this year's semifinalists.The knockout stage has already delivered plenty of drama. Norway eliminated five-time champions Brazil with a 2-1 victory, while Spain edged past Portugal thanks to Mikel Merino's stoppage-time winner. Defending champions Argentina also survived a major scare before fighting back to beat Egypt 3-2.The quarterfinal action begins on Friday with France taking on Morocco at Gillette Stadium in Foxborough. France booked their place with a hard-fought 1-0 victory over Paraguay, while Morocco impressed with a commanding 3-0 win over co-hosts Canada in Houston.Spain and Belgium will contest the second quarterfinal on Saturday. Belgium advanced after defeating the United States 4-1, while Spain head into the contest having yet to concede a goal in the tournament.Sunday features two blockbuster fixtures. Erling Haaland's Norway will face Harry Kane-led England after knocking Brazil out in the previous round, while England edged Mexico 3-2 to secure their place in the last eight.The final quarterfinal sees defending champions Argentina meet Switzerland. Switzerland progressed after defeating Colombia in a dramatic penalty shootout.The four quarterfinals will be played across four stadiums in the United States: Gillette Stadium in Foxborough, SoFi Stadium in Inglewood, Hard Rock Stadium in Miami Gardens and Arrowhead Stadium in Kansas City.The winners of France vs Morocco and Spain vs Belgium will meet in the first semifinal on July 14 at AT&T Stadium in Arlington.The second semifinal will feature the winners of Norway vs England and Argentina vs Switzerland, with a place in the FIFA World Cup 2026 final at stake.
Article Summary: The FIFA World Cup 2026 has reached its business end, with only eight teams remaining in the race for football's biggest prize. The quarterfinal action begins on Friday with France taking on Morocco at Gillette Stadium in Foxborough. Spain and Belgium will contest the second quarterfinal on Saturday. Erling Haaland's Norway will face Harry Kane-led England after knocking Brazil out in the previous round. The final quarterfinal sees defending champions Argentina meet Switzerland.

Article Title: Fact check: Did Kim Kardashian really remove Lewis Hamilton from her social media accounts amid breakup rumors?
Article Text:
Did Kim Kardashian really remove Lewis Hamilton from her social media accounts amid breakup rumors?(Image via getty Images)

Has Kim Kardashian unfollowed Lewis Hamilton from her social media?

Lewis Hamilton refers to his relationship with Kim Kardashian after being private about it

Lewis Hamilton, the popular F1 driver, and high profile reality TV star and SKIMS founder, Kim Kardashian have turned heads with their whirlwind romance in the last few months after they were spotted together at Super Bowl in February. While the couple recently admitted their relationship in public, Kim Kardashian’s alleged social media move has sparked chaos.No, the reality TV star Kim Kardashian has not unfollowed Lewis Hamilton from her social media accounts.The truth is, though Kim Kardashian had posted Lewis Hamilton on her social media last month, the two never really added each other on social media.A few years ago, the F1 legend Lewis Hamilton removed everyone from his social media accounts and only follows his business accounts for now.For the unversed, a few hours ago, a fan page on X made a post that read as, “Kim Kardashian unfollowed Lewis Hamilton.”The post added, “It was claimed that the reason for the breakup was Lewis Hamilton's inability to get over his ex-girlfriend and Kim Kardashian's step-sister Kendall Jenner .”This has sparked chaos as it has divided fans.A fan took to X and posted, “Yeah, it was already a ridiculous relationship, is this guy an idiot or what, Lewis messing around with that woman who has kids, and then going off with those 20/25-year-old hotties.”Another fan posted, “Neither of them even followed each other, it's fake news—even if they broke up, you couldn't figure it out that way.”However, neither Kim Kardashian nor Lewis Hamilton has responded to the speculations yet.Just a few days ago, ahead of the British Grand Prix, Lewis Hamilton was told by an interviewer that he appeared to be a "much happier man this year”,. This led to another person asking, "fast car or new girlfriend?"To this, Lewis Hamilton said, "That's a good one,...Once you spend a year with this team, Ferrari is still the most iconic team of all-time. You know, they've had a difficult period of time. It's such an amazing team, and they've been so welcoming.”Before he met Kim Kardashian, last year in an interview he opened up about his personal life as he said, "Yes, it's very complicated, especially in today's world. I see other drivers and I wonder how they manage: Some have children, are married, have girlfriends. I went through that in my twenties. I made a different choice: I decided to maximize the time I have here because it's shorter than people think. I don't want any regrets, or to think I could have given more. ”
Article Summary: Kim Kardashian has not unfollowed Lewis Hamilton from her social media accounts. A few hours ago, a fan page on X made a post that read as, “Kim Kardashian unfollows Lewis Hamilton.” This has sparked chaos as it has divided fans. Neither Kim Kardashian nor Lewis Hamilton has responded to the speculations.

Article Title: Indian Film Festival of Melbourne 2026 unveils nominations; Aamir Khan, Mammootty, Rishab Shetty and Ranveer Singh lead Best Actor category
Article Text:
Indian Film Festival of Melbourne 2026 unveils nominations; Aamir Khan, Mammootty, Rishab Shetty and Ranveer Singh lead Best Actor category

Strong competition in the Best Film category

Aamir Khan, Mammootty and Rishab Shetty among Best Actor nominees

Streaming and television categories feature popular titles

The 17th edition of the Indian Film Festival of Melbourne (IFFM) has unveiled its nominations, recognising some of the most acclaimed films, performances and filmmakers from across Indian cinema.This year's festival will be held from August 13 to August 23, 2026, with the support of the Victorian Government. Along with its awards ceremony, the event will feature premieres, special screenings, masterclasses, panel discussions, industry interactions and cultural programmes.The race for the Best Film award features productions from multiple languages and genres. The nominated films are Haq, Jarann, Kantara: A Legend - Chapter 1, Lokah Chapter 1: Chandra, Saiyaara, Sitaare Zameen Par, Sharthopor and The Great Shamsuddin Family. 'Kantara: A Legend - Chapter 1' has emerged as one of the notable contenders following its successful theatrical run. The Best Director category is equally competitive. The nominees include Ashwiny Iyer Tiwari, Chandrasish Ray, Mohit Suri, Ramalingam Gowtham, Rima Das, Rishab Shetty, R.S. Prasanna and Shazia Iqbal The Best Performance (Male) category features several of Indian cinema's biggest names. The nominees are Aamir Khan, Ahaan Panday, Bhuman Bhargav Das, Dulquer Salmaan, Mammootty, Ranveer Singh , Rishab Shetty and Sanjay Mishra. Meanwhile, the Best Performance (Female) nominees include Aneet Padda, Kalyani Priyadarshan, Kirti Kulhari, Neena Gupta, Rajshri Deshpande, Rani Mukerji, Rituparna Sengupta and Yami Gautam Dhar. For Rishab Shetty, this year's nominations are especially significant as he has earned recognition both as a filmmaker and as an actor.The festival has also recognised outstanding work in the streaming space. The Best Performance in a Series category includes Ambrish Verma, Arif Zakaria, Barun Sobti, Kunal Kemmu, Paramvir Singh Cheema, Manav Kaul, Manoj Pahwa, Vijay Varma, Anandhi, Huma Qureshi, Lakshmi Priyaa Chandramouli, Mona Singh, Rasika Dugal , Seema Pahwa, Shefali Shah and Swastika Mukherjee. The nominees for Best Series are 'Baai Tujhyapayi', 'Kohrra: Season 2', 'Matka King', 'Real Kashmir Football Club', 'Sapne Vs Everyone', 'The Ba***ds of Bollywood', 'Perfect Family' and 'Freedom at Midnight: Season 2'.The winners across all categories will be decided by an independent jury comprising respected personalities from Australia's film industry and cultural sector. The awards will be presented during the festival's closing ceremony later in August as reported by IANS.
Article Summary: Indian Film Festival of Melbourne 2026 unveils nominations. Aamir Khan, Mammootty, Rishab Shetty and Ranveer Singh lead Best Actor category. Best Director category features Ashwiny Iyer Tiwari, Chandrasish Ray, Mohit Suri and Rima Das.

Article Title: Raiders star Fernando Mendoza hit with damning verdict amid ex-NFL scout's harsh assessment
Article Text:
Raiders rookie Fernando Mendoza hit with damning verdict amid ex-NFL scout's harsh assessment(Getty Image)

Former scout Daniel Kelly questions Fernando Mendoza while Raiders stay focused on development



The Las Vegas Raiders drafted Fernando Mendoza with the No. 1 overall pick because they believe he can become the face of the franchise. Just weeks before training camp, though, the rookie has found himself at the center of a heated debate after one former NFL scout questioned whether he will ever live up to that belief.The strong criticism came even as Mendoza continues to learn the offense and waits to sign his rookie contract. While the Raiders remain patient, the comment quickly grabbed attention across NFL circles and sparked fresh discussion about the quarterback's future.The debate began when ESPN fantasy analyst Eric Karabell wrote on X that the Raiders had "upgraded" their quarterback room by adding Fernando Mendoza. Soon after, former NFL scout Daniel Kelly fired back with a blunt response. "Fernando Mendoza is a guaranteed bust."Kelly posted the remark on X in response to a Sleeper Raiders post highlighting Karabell's opinion. The short message spread quickly and divided fans, especially because Fernando Mendoza has not yet played an NFL snap.The criticism comes after an up-and-down offseason for the rookie. During organized team activities, The Athletic reporter Sam Warren noted that veteran Kirk Cousins handled the first-team reps while Mendoza spent most of his time working with the rookies.Warren also reported that Mendoza's accuracy dipped during the final OTA practice and that he struggled against the Raiders' starting defense.Still, the coaching staff has consistently urged patience. Speaking after an OTA practice in June, Raiders quarterbacks coach Mie Sullivan said Mendoza remains in the early stages of learning the offense. "He's still learning what to do, making sure he knows his job, and then we can start to take some of the next steps, but it's a long road and a lot of work to do. We're pleased with the work that he's been putting in to this point. "The Las Vegas Raiders also have no reason to rush the rookie. Cousins entered the offseason as the experienced option and remains the favorite to open the 2026 season as the starting quarterback. That gives Mendoza valuable time to adjust to NFL speed before taking over the offense.Another talking point involves his contract. As of now, Fernando Mendoza remained one of only two first-round picks from the 2026 NFL Draft without a signed rookie deal, along with Ty Simpson of the Los Angeles Rams. According to Las Vegas Raiders insider Q Myers, the delay centers on the payment schedule of Mendoza's signing bonus rather than the total value of the contract. Myers added that the expectation around the team remains that a deal will get done before training camp.Head coach Klint Kubiak echoed that approach after offseason workouts. He said the real evaluation begins during the 11 training camp practices before the first preseason game.For now, Daniel Kelly's "guaranteed bust" prediction remains just that, a prediction. The Las Vegas Raiders will judge Fernando Mendoza on what happens during training camp and on the field, not on one offseason opinion.
Article Summary: Fernando Mendoza is one of only two first-round picks from the 2026 NFL Draft without a signed rookie deal. Article Title: Nolan Wells update: What happened to Mississippi teen after Horn Island trip? Family takes new step for investigation
Article Text:
Nolan Wells update: What happened to Mississippi teen after Horn Island trip? Family takes new step for investigation (Image via Getty)

Who was Nolan Xavier Wells and what happened during the Horn Island trip ?

Why has Nolan Xavier Wells’ family hired attorney Ben Crump?

Eighteen-year-old Nolan Xavier Wells, a student-athlete from Ocean Springs, Mississippi, was found dead after going missing during a Fourth of July trip to Horn Island. His family is now seeking answers and has hired civil rights attorney Ben Crump as investigators continue to look into what happened. Authorities have not yet determined a cause of death, and an autopsy was scheduled for July 7. The case has drawn attention across Mississippi as family members, friends, and community members wait for more information about the final hours of the young football player’s life.According to the Jackson County Sheriff’s Department, Wells traveled by boat to Horn Island with a group of friends on July 4 to celebrate Independence Day. Officials said he was last seen on the island at around 3 p.m.When the group returned, Wells was not with them. His mother reported him missing later that night, triggering a large search operation involving local, state, and federal agencies. The search included the U.S. Coast Guard, the Mississippi Department of Marine Resources, and the National Park Service.On the morning of July 6, a U.S. park ranger found a body near the northwestern tip of Horn Island.Wells’ family later confirmed that it was Nolan.Jackson County Sheriff John Ledbetter told The Associated Press that investigators had spoken with people who were with Wells on the island and that they were cooperating with the investigation.“From the people we’ve talked to, it sounds like he chose to stay on the island with the assumption that he was going to ride back to the mainland with someone else,” Ledbetter told The Associated Press.Officials have stressed that the investigation remains active and that no official cause of death has been announced.On July 7, Ben Crump’s law office announced that Wells’ family had retained the nationally known attorney to help them seek answers about the teenager’s death.In a statement released by the legal team, Crump said the family deserves a full and transparent investigation into the circumstances surrounding Wells’ death.“Nolan Wells was a beloved son, teammate and friend who went out to celebrate the Fourth of July and never came home. His family deserves answers. They deserve the truth. We will not rest until every fact about what happened to Nolan on Horn Island is brought into the light, and we call on investigators to pursue this case with the urgency and transparency this family deserves.”The legal team said it plans to conduct an independent review while also pushing for the release of records, witness statements, and autopsy findings.Wells was a football player at Ocean Springs High School and later became a wide receiver at Southwest Mississippi Community College. He was preparing to return for the upcoming season and would have turned 19 next month.His mother, Christine Wonsley, also appealed for information through social media, asking anyone who may have videos or other material from Horn Island to come forward.For now, many questions remain unanswered. Investigators are continuing their work, while Wells’ family says it is focused on learning exactly what happened during the holiday trip that ended in tragedy.
Article Summary: Nolan Xavier Wells, 18, was found dead on July 6 after going missing during a Fourth of July trip to Horn Island. Authorities have not yet determined a cause of death, and an autopsy was scheduled for July 7. Wells was a football player at Ocean Springs High School and later became a wide receiver at Southwest Mississippi Community College.

Article Title: "Seven absolutely incredible artists": Global Citizen CEO showers praise on BTS and ARMY ahead of FIFA World Cup 2026 halftime show
Article Text:
Global Citizen CEO Hugh Evans thanked BTS and ARMY ahead of the FIFA World Cup 2026 final halftime show, revealing fans have already raised over $40,000 for the FIFA Global Citizen Education Fund. He praised BTS for using their platform to support children's education worldwide. The historic halftime show on July 19 will feature BTS, Madonna and Shakira, with proceeds helping the campaign's $100 million fundraising goal.

BTS FIFA World Cup 2026 halftime show inspires ARMY to support children's education

BTS joins Shakira and Madonna for historic FIFA World Cup 2026 halftime show

One of the biggest talking-points ahead of the FIFA World Cup 2026 final comes from a simple social media post. As excitement builds for BTS’ historic halftime show, Global Citizen CEO Hugh Evans has publicly thanked the K-pop stars’ fans, after revealing ARMY has already raised more than $40,000 for children’s education. His message has shifted the focus away from the stage, showing how the next show is already impacting the real world weeks before kickoff.Global Citizen CEO Hugh Evans posted an emotional message and artwork of BTS members on social media, thanking the group and its global fanbase for supporting the FIFA Global Citizen Education Fund.He said in the post that BTS will use the FIFA World Cup 2026 Final Halftime Show to highlight children’s education and encourage fans to donate to the campaign. “Even before the performance starts, ARMY has already made a meaningful difference,” Evans said.He said: "Seven absolutely incredible artists who chose to give back to children around the world. BTS is taking time away from their global tour to stand on the biggest stage in history. The first ever FIFA World Cup final halftime show on July 19. In support of the FIFA Global Citizen Education Fund and our mission to raise 100 million dollars for children's education worldwide.""And ARMY, you haven't just waited for July 19 to show up. You've already raised over $40,000 for the fund. That's books, teachers, classrooms, safe places to play. That's children's whose lives are going to be changed because of you. BTS, thank you for choosing Purpose at this moment. For using your platform and your voices for kids who need it most. This community is one of the most powerful forces for good in the world. And on behalf of Global Citizen and the FIFA Global Citizen Education Fund, we thank you. Thank you."Evans also posted separately that ARMY had already crossed the $40,000 milestone before July 19, calling the donations a direct investment in books, teachers, classrooms and opportunities for children worldwide.The upcoming FIFA World Cup final will make history by introducing the tournament's first-ever Super Bowl-style halftime show. The performance is scheduled for July 19 at MetLife Stadium, where football and music will share the spotlight during the championship match.BTS will headline the event alongside pop icon Madonna and Colombian superstar Shakira. The show is being curated by Coldplay frontman Chris Martin and is expected to support the FIFA Global Citizen Education Fund, which aims to raise $100 million to improve access to education for children around the world.FIFA President Gianni Infantino previously described the event as an opportunity to bring together "music and football on the biggest stage in sport for a very special cause."For BTS, the performance marks another milestone in the group's World Cup journey. It comes four years after Jung Kook performed Dreamers during the opening ceremony of the 2022 FIFA World Cup in Qatar. This time, all seven members will take the stage together in what is expected to be one of the most-watched live musical performances in history.As the countdown to July 19 continues, the conversation is extending far beyond entertainment. Thanks to BTS' involvement and the generosity of ARMY, the halftime show has already begun making an impact before a single note has been performed.
Article Summary: Global Citizen CEO Hugh Evans thanks BTS and ARMY. Reveals fans have already raised over $40,000 for the FIFA Global Citizen Education Fund. The historic halftime show on July 19 will feature BTS, Madonna and Shakira, with proceeds helping the campaign's $100 million fundraising goal.

Article Title: Ronaldo effect? FIFA World Cup ticket prices crash after USA, Portugal exits
Article Text:
Portugal's Cristiano Ronaldo (7) (AP Photo)

The FIFA World Cup 2026 quarterfinals are set to begin this week, but ticket prices for one of the biggest knockout matches have dropped significantly following the elimination of co-host United States and Portugal.According to secondary marketplace TickPick, the get-in price for Friday's quarterfinal between Spain and Belgium has fallen dramatically from around $2,950 to nearly $1,200 as of Tuesday afternoon.The sharp decline comes after the United States suffered a 4-1 defeat to Belgium in the Round of 16, ending the co-hosts' World Cup campaign. A potential quarterfinal involving the home side would likely have generated much greater demand among fans.Portugal's exit also played a major role in the falling prices. Spain's 1-0 victory over Portugal not only ended Cristiano Ronaldo 's FIFA World Cup career but also removed one of the tournament's biggest global attractions from the knockout stages.A quarterfinal featuring the United States and Portugal had been viewed as one of the marquee possibilities of the tournament, with the presence of Ronaldo and the host nation expected to drive ticket demand even higher.Meanwhile, France's quarterfinal against Morocco at Gillette Stadium in Foxborough is currently the least expensive last-eight fixture on the secondary market. According to TickPick, tickets for the match are available from $989.The quarterfinals get underway on Friday with France taking on Morocco after Didier Deschamps' side edged Paraguay 1-0 in the Round of 16, while Morocco advanced with an emphatic 3-0 victory over co-hosts Canada.Spain will meet Belgium in the second quarterfinal after the Red Devils eliminated the United States 4-1, while Spain remain the only team yet to concede a goal in this year's tournament.Sunday's action begins with Erling Haaland's Norway facing Harry Kane-led England before defending champions Argentina take on Switzerland in the final quarterfinal.The four last-eight matches will be staged across Gillette Stadium in Foxborough, SoFi Stadium in Inglewood, Hard Rock Stadium in Miami Gardens and Arrowhead Stadium in Kansas City, with the winners advancing to next week's semifinals.
Article Summary: FIFA World Cup 2026 quarterfinals set to begin this week. Ticket prices for one of the biggest knockout matches have dropped significantly following the elimination of co-hosts United States and Portugal. The get-in price for Friday's quarterfinal between Spain and Belgium has fallen dramatically from around $2,950 to nearly $1,200 as of Tuesday afternoon.

Article Title: Former England striker Wayne Rooney says Portugal players don't support Cristiano Ronaldo like Argentina backs Lionel Messi
Article Text:
Wayne Rooney believes Portugal players did not support Cristiano Ronaldo the way Argentina rallies behind Lionel Messi during the 2026 FIFA World Cup. After Portugal's 1-0 Round of 16 loss to Spain, Rooney praised Argentina's unity around Messi while questioning Portugal's team dynamic. Ronaldo, who confirmed it was his final World Cup, finished with three goals before Portugal coach Roberto Martinez announced his departure.

Wayne Rooney compares Cristiano Ronaldo and Lionel Messi after Portugal's World Cup exit

Cristiano Ronaldo's final World Cup ends with disappointment

Portugal's FIFA World Cup campaign ended with more questions than answers, and Wayne Rooney believes the biggest issue goes beyond tactics. After Portugal's 1-0 Round of 16 defeat to Spain, the former England captain suggested Cristiano Ronaldo lacked the same level of backing from his teammates that Lionel Messi continues to receive from Argentina, drawing a sharp comparison between football's two greatest modern rivals.Portugal's elimination came after a frustrating performance against Spain, with Ronaldo enduring another difficult night in what he has already confirmed will be his final FIFA World Cup. The 41-year-old completed the full 90 minutes but managed only 19 touches and three shots, while striker Gonçalo Ramos remained on the bench despite scoring the winner against Croatia in the previous round.Ronaldo finished the tournament with three goals. Two came against Uzbekistan, while his other goal was a penalty against Croatia. However, his overall displays drew criticism as Portugal failed to make the most of one of their strongest squads in recent years.Speaking on BBC after the match, Rooney pointed to a major difference between Portugal and defending champions Argentina."You always put Ronaldo and Messi together because they are two of the best players the game has ever seen," Rooney said on BBC."But the Argentinian lads seem like they're all there for Messi.They're all behind him. Messi has obviously been fantastic at this World Cup. I don't get that vibe from the Portuguese players."Rooney's comments immediately fueled debate among football fans, especially as Messi continues to lead Argentina's title defense with seven goals in the tournament.Portugal entered the 2026 World Cup carrying high expectations thanks to a squad packed with elite talent. Players including Nuno Mendes, João Neves and Vitinha arrived after helping Paris Saint-Germain win the UEFA Champions League, while Bruno Fernandes had just been named the Premier League Player of the Season.Despite that quality, Portugal struggled to translate its talent into consistent performances on the biggest stage. Rooney's observations suggested the issue may have been less about individual ability and more about the chemistry around Ronaldo.Following the defeat, Ronaldo reflected on the end of his World Cup journey with an emotional message."I'm sad to leave the World Cup this way," Ronaldo said post-match. "As I said yesterday, I gave it my all, and I leave with a clear conscience. That's the life of a footballer. You have to move forward."It was my last World Cup, yes. But as for the rest, there's time to think, to be with my family, and not say things in the heat of the moment."Portugal's disappointing exit also brought changes on the sidelines, with head coach Roberto Martinez confirming he would step down from his role.For Ronaldo, the defeat marked the end of an extraordinary World Cup career that spanned decades and produced countless memorable moments. Rooney's comparison with Messi will likely continue to spark discussion, particularly as Argentina remain in the race for another world title. Whether Portugal lacked the same unity Rooney described is open to interpretation, but their early elimination has ensured every aspect of their campaign will remain under the spotlight for some time.
Article Summary: Wayne Rooney compares Cristiano Ronaldo and Lionel Messi after Portugal's World Cup exit. Rooney believes Portugal players did not support Ronaldo the way Argentina rallied behind Messi. Ronaldo confirmed it was his final World Cup after scoring three goals in Brazil. Portugal coach Roberto Martinez announced he would step down from his role.

Article Title: 'How has Vaibhav Sooryavanshi not played?' : Ex-England captain baffled by India's decision
Article Text:
India's Vaibhav Sooryavanshi (ANI Photo)

Poll Vote & Share your view Who should be dropped to accommodate Sooryavanshi in the starting XI? Abhishek Sharma Sanju Samson 3k+ users shared opinion today 5k+ users already voted today 3k+ users shared opinion today Share Opinion

The excitement around Vaibhav Sooryavanshi has gone far beyond India, with the 15-year-old attracting attention from cricket fans across the world. Such has been the hype surrounding the teenage sensation that tickets for India's T20Is in Ireland were snapped up by supporters hoping to watch him in action. However, Sooryavanshi remained on the sidelines throughout the two-match series.The story did not change after India travelled to Durham for the T20Is against England. Despite the growing anticipation around his international debut, the youngster once again had to settle for a place on the bench. His continued omission has left several former cricketers puzzled, including Ravi Shastri and Sunil Gavaskar . Now, former England captains Michael Vaughan and Alastair Cook have also questioned India's decision.Speaking on the Stick to Cricket podcast, Vaughan admitted he could not understand why Sooryavanshi had not been given an opportunity, even suggesting he would like to put the question directly to the BCCI and chairman of selectors Ajit Agarkar.“We must say to the BCCI and the selector, Ajit, how has he not played against Ireland? Our mate, the Kid,” said Vaughan on the Stick to Cricket podcast, to which Cook replied: “I’m there tomorrow, and I’m driving up as a fan to make sure that the 15-year-old kid plays.”While Vaughan and Cook's comments reflected the growing curiosity over Sooryavanshi's absence, the chances of the Indian team management changing course appear slim. The selectors and coaching staff have consistently maintained that the teenager's opportunity will come at the appropriate time.At present, Sooryavanshi's path into the playing XI is blocked by Abhishek Sharma and Sanju Samson , both of whom remain firmly established in India's T20I side. Having played key roles in India's third T20 World Cup triumph four months ago, neither batter is expected to lose his place anytime soon.Even so, Sooryavanshi's omission has continued to spark debate considering the extraordinary rise he has enjoyed over the past five months. The left-hander first captained India Under-19 to the World Cup title, smashing a breathtaking 175 in the final. He then carried that momentum into the IPL, piling up 776 runs to become the youngest-ever Orange Cap winner.Before joining the senior squad for the Ireland tour, Sooryavanshi also impressed for India A in Sri Lanka. His match-winning 94 in the final included the fastest half-century in List A cricket and played a decisive role in India's comprehensive victory, further strengthening the case of those eager to see the teenage prodigy make his international debut.
Article Summary: India's Vaibhav Sooryavanshi is yet to make his international debut. The 15-year-old was left on the bench for India's T20Is against England. Michael Vaughan and Alastair Cook have questioned the selectors' decision. Abhishek Sharma and Sanju Samson remain firmly established in the side.

Article Title: 'Tougher days will come': Ex-India cricketer's reality check for Vaibhav Sooryavanshi
Article Text:
Vaibhav Sooryavanshi (Image: IANS)

India face England in Manchester

Former India wicketkeeper-batter Dinesh Karthik has lavished praise on teenage sensation Vaibhav Sooryavanshi, calling him a rare talent capable of rewriting T20 records, while also urging the youngster to stay grounded amid the growing spotlight.The 15-year-old batting prodigy has emerged as one of India's brightest prospects after his remarkable rise in domestic and IPL. He is likely to make his senior India debut during the ongoing T20I series against England, expectations around Sooryavanshi continue to soar.Speaking about the youngster's potential in Sky Sports, Karthik highlighted Sooryavanshi's fearless mindset and ability to score freely against any opposition."I think it's just his ability to score all around the ground and the tempo at which he bats. The fact that he doesn't get fazed by whoever his opponent is stands out. That fearlessness is a special quality," Karthik said.While acknowledging the teenager's immense talent, Karthik reminded everyone that every young cricketer goes through challenging phases, both on and off the field."Like everybody in life, I'm sure he will face challenges with his batting and things that happen away from cricket.He needs to learn how to deal with those situations. Right now, everything looks rosy, but tougher days will come," he added.Asked how far Sooryavanshi could go in the game, Karthik left little room for doubt."The sky is the limit. I genuinely feel he can become an extraordinary T20 batter, and I don't see why he can't break many records in the format," he said."My one piece of advice would be to learn to deal with the noise off the field in a healthy manner because that's going to be his biggest challenge in today's world," Karthik concluded.India and England will look to break the deadlock when they meet in the second T20I at Old Trafford, Manchester, after the series opener in Durham was washed out by rain. Despite the no result, India would have taken confidence from posting a competitive 189/7, powered by half-centuries from Abhishek Sharma and captain Shreyas Iyer.
Article Summary: Former India wicketkeeper-batter Dinesh Karthik has lavished praise on teenage sensation Vaibhav Sooryavanshi. The 15-year-old batting prodigy has emerged as one of India's brightest prospects. He is likely to make his senior India debut during the ongoing T20I series.

Article Title: “Three-against-one situation”: El Rubius opens up on being “targeted” in MrBeast’s viral $1M challenge
Article Text:
El Rubius reacted honestly after his elimination from MrBeast’s $1 million challenge, calling it “unfair” and claiming he was targeted in a three-against-one situation. Despite his frustration, he thanked fans and gave away his $50,000 winnings. His response felt real and balanced, especially after MrBeast’s team later sent him a cake, softening the moment.

El Rubius reflects on a tough and “unfair” exit







El Rubius did not just walk away from MrBeast’s $1 million streamer challenge. He opened up. Soon after his elimination, the Spanish creator shared a raw reaction with fans, giving them a closer look at what the experience felt like from inside the game. It was not polished or carefully framed. It felt real, and that is exactly why it caught attention so quickly.The clip and his response have since fueled conversations across the streaming space. Some viewers believe intense competitions like this naturally lead to uneven situations. Others feel Rubius may have had a point about being singled out. Either way, his words have added a human layer to a challenge that many only saw as entertainment.Speaking through an Instagram Story, Rubius did not hide his frustration, but he also made sure to thank his supporters. He said, "Guys, I just got knocked out of the game. It was a bit of an unfair elimination, but hey, at least I managed to walk away with $50,000 for you all, which I’ve already distributed. It went to the mods, the subs, and the non-subs alike."He further added, "I’m happy, of course, though admittedly, it was incredibly tough.I wasn't really a fan of the challenge itself, either. But then again... it was basically a three-against-one situation the entire time. I mean, they were targeting me specifically the whole way through. I kept trying to go after RaKai, but, well, he actually just got eliminated in the end. So, at least justice was served for Abraham. Abraham, if you’re watching this, we were all rooting for you, and standing right there with you."His message carried mixed emotions. There was disappointment, yes, but also pride in how he handled things. Giving away his $50,000 winnings to his community stood out as a gesture that meant more than the result itself.He continued with another honest note, saying, "And in the end, karma finally caught up with that little punk. Sorry, I’m being a bit toxic right now, but let's just hope the MrBeast team doesn't see this! Guys, thank you all so much. Seriously, thank you for all the support and for just being there."He added, "Every time I asked for something in the chat or whatever it was, you guys were right there. You’re incredible. I’m a little disappointed in myself, personally, mostly for not performing better, but hey, I gave it my all, and we still walked away with $50,000, so we’ve got to look on the bright side. All right, guys, big hugs to all my brothers out there!"Interestingly, the story did not end with frustration. Later that day, Rubius revealed that MrBeast’s team had sent him a cake after his exit. It was a small gesture, but it shifted the mood. It showed that even in a high-pressure challenge, there is still room for respect.In the end, Rubius’ reaction felt honest and grounded. That is what stayed with people.
Article Summary: El Rubius was eliminated from MrBeast's $1 million streamer challenge. He called it "unfair" and said he was targeted in a three-against-one situation. Despite his frustration, he thanked fans and gave away his $50,000 winnings.

Article Title: Ipl Ticket Scam: IPL craze costs techie Rs 1.46 lakh in fake RCB vs CSK ticket scam
Article Text:
Image credit: AI generated pic

Watch Devdutt Padikkal press conference: Chinnaswamy pitch, Tim David’s power & RCB’s batting strategy Devdutt Padikkal press conference: Chinnaswamy pitch, Tim David’s power & RCB’s batting strategy

RCB register their second win

Poll Do you think the demand for IPL tickets is too high every season? Yes, it's excessive. No, it's just right.

NEW DELHI: IPL comes and the demand for tickets and passes go soaring up by the fans. The desperation to get inside the stadium to watch their favourite teams in action in the IPL go high and high. When the tickets get sold out, the demand gets too hig, and the cricket fans even ask for tickets in black. But for a 25-year-old techie the craze for IPL tickets for RCB vs CSK turned too costly and he was duped for a massive Rs. 1.46 lakh.The techie, a resident of B Narayanapura, fell into a trap by a post posted by a fraudster who posed himself as a ticket seller on Instagram.As per a report in Deccan Herald, the techie alleged in a complaint to Mahadevapura Police that an individual claiming to be Sumit Biswal promised he could arrange IPL tickets along with food coupons.As per the techie, Biswal was introduced him as a senior supervisor for ticket counter at M Chinnaswamy Stadium"He asked me to come near the stadium gate number 10, saying someone would deliver the tickets within minutes, and even sent an email confirmation to gain my trust," the complainant told Deccan Herald."Initially, I agreed to buy two tickets for Rs 3,700 each,but he kept asking for more money under various pretexts such as refundable security deposits, additional ID cards and food coupons.Trusting him, I made multiple payments - even using my mother’s bank account after exhausting my own limits—and ended up transferring around Rs 1.46 lakh," he added.Bhuvneshwar Kumar’s incisive spell of 3-41, coming after a marauding batting show from Tim David, Rajat Patidar and Devdutt Padikkal, propelled Royal Challengers Bengaluru (RCB) to a commanding 43-run victory over Chennai Super Kings (CSK) in their IPL 2026 encounter at the M Chinnaswamy Stadium on Sunday.The contest saw RCB’s batting unit dismantle CSK’s attack with relentless hitting, as David’s 70 not out off just 25 balls and Patidar’s unbeaten 48 off only 19 balls provided for the late fireworks after Padikkal’s fluent 50 off 29 balls had set the tone for the daunting total.Their combined effort lifted RCB to a daunting 250/3, thus setting a new record for highest total in IPL 2026. In reply, CSK faltered under scoreboard pressure, with Sarfaraz Khan’s 50 off 24 balls and Prashant Veer’s 43 the lone acts of resistance amid a string of failures from the top order.Bhuvneshwar was at his disciplined best and even crossed the 200-mark in terms of wickets in IPL, with others also chipping in as CSK were bowled out for 207 in 19.4 overs. The comprehensive win also meant RCB have registered four consecutive triumphs over CSK for the first time in the IPL’s history.
Article Summary: Royal Challengers Bengaluru (RCB) beat Chennai Super Kings (CSK) in their IPL 2026 encounter at the M Chinnaswamy Stadium on Sunday. Tim David, Rajat Patidar and Devdutt Padikkal put on a marauding batting show to lift RCB to a daunting 250/3. RCB have registered four consecutive triumphs over CSK for the first time in the IPL's history.

Article Title: Clavicular Viral Controversy: "Trans women are.....": Clavicular’s viral moment with trans women sparks fresh conversation on internet culture
Article Text:
A viral clip featuring Clavicular and trans women is gaining attention for its calm and honest tone. The conversation explores how online language and trends move across communities. Instead of conflict, the exchange highlights shared influence and mutual awareness. Viewers are engaging with its thoughtful nature, as it reflects how internet culture evolves through collaboration rather than ownership.

Clavicular and trans women share a surprising realization about online trends







The video of Clavicular, a short-video producer, is going around the Internet, yet not the reason why. There is no yelling or over-the-top drama. It is a mere, everyday talk that seems almost novel nowadays, in the age of internet, which is overflowing with content. The scene is easy-going, the tone of the narrative is straightforward, and the characters appear more concerned with conversation than combat.The message that is being addressed is the flow of the conversation. It discusses internet culture, the way people communicate with one another and the place of trends. Rather than bringing out a debate, the encounter towards curiosity. It quietly brings attention to something that a lot of people who use the internet notice but don't often say out loud. People in different communities often have a bigger impact on each other than they think.The conversation begins with a confident and lighthearted comment from one of the trans women present. She says, "You know that trans women are like the OG lip masters, right? We are very talented in the program. Like, I do the peptides, I'm on Zeta." Her tone carries a mix of pride and humor, setting a relaxed vibe at the table.Clavicular responds in a way that keeps the mood steady but adds a different angle.He says, "That is funny because you guys were using a lot of the words in like the South Korean subreddits in like 2014." It’s not confrontational. If anything, it feels like an observation. He points to how certain words and ideas may have traveled across different online spaces over time.Another voice joins in and brings the moment full circle. "Guys, we're like the OGs. I saw a couple of posts about that. We're like copying each other." That line hits in a simple but honest way. It reflects something familiar. Online culture is rarely created in isolation. It grows through sharing, borrowing, and sometimes even unknowingly repeating.That's what makes the clip so powerful. It doesn't want to make a point or win an argument. It only shows a group of people talking, listening, and figuring something out together.This moment feels different in a space where viral content often leads to fights. It reminds people that conversations can be calm, thoughtful, and still important. And sometimes, that honest silence is what makes it work.
Article Summary: A viral clip featuring Clavicular and trans women is gaining attention for its calm and honest tone. The conversation explores how online language and trends move across communities. Viewers are engaging with its thoughtful nature, as it reflects how internet culture evolves through collaboration.

Article Title: Mumbai's 7-day rainfall exceeds Delhi's annual average, nearly matches Bengaluru
Article Text:
Mumbai recorded its wettest July days in recent years with intense downpours.





MUMBAI: Mumbai received as much rain between June 30 and July 6 as several major metros typically do in an entire year.IMD's Colaba observatory recorded 882. 6mm of rain and Santacruz 988. 8mm during the seven-day period, surpassing the average annual rainfall of Delhi and Pune and nearly matching Bengaluru's yearly total.For perspective, Delhi's average annual rainfall is around 774mm, meaning Colaba received the equivalent of 113% of the national capital's yearly rainfall in just a week.The rainfall also exceeded Pune's annual average of 720mm and nearly matched Bengaluru's 986mm. The heaviest rainfall was recorded on July 4, when Colaba logged 265. 6mm and Santacruz 227. 7mm in 24 hours, making it one of the city's wettest July days in recent years.Read More: Mumbai Rain Live Updates Municipal commissioner Ashwini Bhide said the heavy rain was accompanied by sustained winds of 40-50 kmph, with gusts reaching 70-80 kmph in some parts of the city.She said all six pumping stations and 10 mini pumping stations were operational, while dewatering pumps were deployed wherever required to clear waterlogged stretches.Bhide said the civic administration came across media reports showing waterlogging at certain locations, including outside actor Amitabh Bachchan's residence, but maintained they did not reflect the actual situation on the ground."We were monitoring these locations live through the disaster control room, and the reports did not match the real-time conditions at the site," she said.The rain intensity eased on Tuesday, prompting the IMD to downgrade its warning for Mumbai from an orange alert to yellow. Between 8. 30am and 5. 30pm, Colaba recorded 0. 7mm of rainfall and Santacruz 6. 1mm. Moderate rainfall is expected over the next few days.
Article Summary: Mumbai received as much rain between June 30 and July 6 as several major metros typically do in an entire year. IMD's Colaba observatory recorded 882. 6mm of rain and Santacruz 988. 8mm during the seven-day period. Heavy rain was accompanied by sustained winds of 40-50 kmph.

Article Title: Body of Nalasopara man found alongside Lonavla-Karjat railway tracks
Article Text: Navi Mumbai: The body of a Nalasopara resident, Chandrakant Chavhan, 55, was retrieved off the railway tracks near Monkey Hill on the Lonavla-Karjat section on Tuesday evening. Under the guidance of Khopoli police inspector Sachin Hire, the volunteers of local NGO Help Foundation brought the body amid inclement weather conditions, including incessant rain, by trekking around 9 km.Prior to mobilisation, the rescue team members were advised to take a basket stretcher, body bag, surgical mask and gloves, helmet, ropes, water, walkie-talkie, sanitisers, among others, as the body was in a decomposed condition and lying in a high-risk area prone to boulder fall and landslides.Chavhan's family members reached the spot and were accompanying the rescuers along with the body. The rescuers scaled down to pull out his body.Inspector Sachin Hire said, “Prima facie he was suspected to have fallen from a train more than two days ago. Further details can be revealed after collating more information from his family members.”Chavhan went missing while travelling from Mumbai to Solapur on the night of July 4. He was last seen around 2.30 am during the journey. No information regarding his whereabouts was received by his family thereafter. A missing person complaint was lodged with the police.
Article Summary: The body of Chandrakant Chavhan, 55, was retrieved off the railway tracks near Monkey Hill on the Lonavla-Karjat section on Tuesday evening. He went missing while travelling from Mumbai to Solapur on the night of July 4.
